target.name: (R)-(−)-Eliprodil
target.motivation: Synthesis of the enantiomer of the neuroprotective drug eliprodil, demonstrating an efficient four-step synthesis including the first earth-abundant metal-catalyzed asymmetric hydrogenation of an α-hydroxy ketone.

success_criteria.free_text: Efficient synthesis in four steps with high enantioselectivity.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-N bond formation between the piperidine nitrogen and the benzylic carbon of the epoxide
key_disconnections.reasoning: This disconnection allows for the introduction of the piperidine moiety and the phenyl group from readily available precursors.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-O bond formation to form the epoxide ring
key_disconnections.reasoning: The epoxide can be formed from a diol, which in turn can be obtained from an α-hydroxy ketone.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-O bond formation to form the α-hydroxy ketone
key_disconnections.reasoning: This is the key chirality-inducing step, allowing for the enantioselective synthesis of the α-hydroxy ketone from a prochiral ketone.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of (R)-(−)-Eliprodil was designed to be efficient and enantioselective, with the key step being an earth-abundant metal-catalyzed asymmetric hydrogenation to establish the crucial stereocenter. Subsequent steps involve standard functional group transformations and ring-opening reactions to construct the final molecule.

thought.background: The target molecule is (R)-(−)-Eliprodil. The first step aims to introduce the initial stereocenter via asymmetric hydrogenation.
thought.gap: Achieving high enantioselectivity in the hydrogenation of the α-hydroxy ketone using an earth-abundant metal catalyst.
thought.decision: To achieve enantioselective reduction, chose Co(OAc)2 with (S,S)-Ph-BPE ligand and salicylic acid additive under H2 atmosphere, expecting high yield and enantioselectivity.

outcome.observation: Asymmetric hydrogenation of 38a afforded diol 38b in 98% yield with 92% ee.

evidence.text_span: Asymmetric hydrogenation of 38a $( {  f C o } ( {  f O A c } ) _ { 2 }$ $( 6 \mathrm { m o l\% } )$ ), (S,S)-Ph-BPE (5 mol%) with salicylic acid $\left( 1 0 ~ \mathrm { m o l\% } ight)$ , $\mathrm { H } _ { 2 }$ (50 bar), toluene, $7 0 ^ { \circ } \mathrm { C }$ , 48 h) resulted in diol 38b ( $9 8 \%$ yield, $9 2 \% \mathrm { e e } ]$

thought.background: Diol 38b has been synthesized with good enantioselectivity. The next step is to convert one of the hydroxyl groups into a leaving group to facilitate epoxide formation.
thought.gap: Selective tosylation of one hydroxyl group in the presence of another.
thought.decision: To selectively tosylate the primary alcohol, chose p-TsCl, DBTO, and TEA in DCM.

evidence.text_span: followed by its tosylation $\dot { p }$ -TsCl, DBTO, TEA, DCM, rt) to obtain 38c

thought.background: Compound 38c contains a tosylate and a hydroxyl group. The next step is to form the epoxide ring.
thought.gap: Efficient intramolecular cyclization to form the epoxide.
thought.decision: To form the epoxide, chose base-mediated epoxidation using NaOH.

evidence.text_span: whose base-mediated epoxidation (NaOH, $\mathrm { Et } _ { 2 } \mathrm { O } ]$ afforded 38d.

thought.background: Epoxide 38d has been formed. The final step is to open the epoxide with the piperidine derivative to yield the target molecule.
thought.gap: Regioselective ring-opening of the epoxide with the amine.
thought.decision: To complete the synthesis, chose ring-opening of 38d with 4-(4-fluorobenzyl)piperidine under reflux conditions.

outcome.observation: Ring-opening of 38d with 4-(4-fluorobenzyl)piperidine delivered (R)-(−)-eliprodil 38e in 64% yield with 97% ee.

evidence.text_span: Finally, the ring-opening of 38d with 4-(4-uorobenzyl)piperidine (i-PrOH, reux, 4 h) delivered (R)-( )-eliprodil 38e in $6 4 \%$ yield with 97%ee (Scheme 38).

validation.significance: This synthesis provides an efficient and enantioselective route to (R)-(−)-Eliprodil, a neuroprotective drug enantiomer, utilizing an earth-abundant metal-catalyzed asymmetric hydrogenation as a key step. This highlights the potential of cobalt catalysis in accessing complex chiral molecules.

source.title: Cobalt catalysis in organic synthesis: a powerful tool in the synthesis of natural products and pharmaceutically active compounds.
source.authors: Mehak I, Khan SG, Noreen R, Hussain SM, Al-Qawasmeh RA, Abbas M, Ali KG, Parveen B, Ashraf R, Zahoor AF.
